content.text: The Maintenance module shall contain tabs: All, Pending Assignment, In Progress, Review, Done/Closed.
the Maintenance module to display workflow tabs
I can navigate between different task stages easily.
the user opens the Maintenance module
the system shall display tabs: All, Pending Assignment, In Progress, Review, Done/Closed.
When the user clicks Done tab, the system shall display only those issues whose status = “Done”.
view only tasks that are completed
I can review issues already resolved.
the user clicks the Done tab
only tasks with status Done shall be displayed.
The Done list shall show columns: Priority, Observation ID (link), Rack Name, Bay ID, Element, Activity Phase, Observation, Action Performed, closed on, Closed by
the Done tab to show relevant completed-task information
I can understand who completed the task and when.
The table shall support pagination with a Rows per page selector and page navigation controls.
pagination controls in the Done list
I can navigate large datasets efficiently.
the Done list contains more rows than one page
the system shall provide a Rows per page selector and page navigation controls.

The system shall provide a Filter button in the header. On click, a filter drawer (defined separately) shall open on the right side, allowing users to filter by Severity, Rack, Bay, Element Type, Activity Phase, Observation Type, closed by, Closed on etc.
a filter drawer to refine the Done list
I can quickly locate specific completed tasks.
the user clicks the Filter button
Severity, Rack, Bay, Element Type, Activity Phase, Observation Type, Closed By, Closed On, etc.
Priority shall be shown as color-coded flags (e.g., red = critical, yellow = medium, green = low).
I can quickly judge the severity of completed tasks.
priority shall appear as colored flags (e.g., red = critical, yellow = medium, green = low).
Observation ID shall be displayed as clickable links (e.g., TSK-0141).
the Observation ID to be clickable
I can open the detailed task information easily.
the user clicks an Observation ID
the system shall open the Task Details screen in a new view.
The Action performed column shall display context buttons such as: Repair, Replace, Under Observation based on task type & status.
the Action Performed column to indicate final actions
I can understand what was done to resolve the issue.
it shall show context buttons such as Repair, Replace, Under Observation, depending on final status.
Closed on column shall show the date when the task is done.
the Closed-On column to show the date the task was completed
I can track the timeliness of issue resolution.
it shall show the completion date for each task.
Task status shall follow a predefined workflow: Pending Assignment → In Progress → Review → Done.
tasks to follow a structured workflow
the lifecycle of a task is clear and consistent.
Pending Assignment → In Progress → Review → Done
System shall display a Group By dropdown with grouping option Action Performed, Closed by, Priority, Location, Activity phase.
I can group completed tasks meaningfully.
the Group By dropdown is available
it shall include options such as:
Action Performed, Closed By, Priority, Location, Activity Phase
Selected group option shall regroup the task list instantly without page reload.
I can analyze grouped results without delay.
the user selects a Group By option
the Done list shall regroup instantly without page refresh.
System shall display a Sort By dropdown in the header on the Maintenance screen across all tabs.
a Sort By dropdown on all Maintenance tabs
I can reorder results consistently across screens.
the user opens any tab in the Maintenance module
the system shall display a Sort By dropdown.
Sorting options include: Closed on, Priority, Action Performed, Rack-> Bay order and Activity phase.
sorting options tailored for the Done tab
I can reorder completed tasks based on meaningful attributes.
The observation list shall re-sort immediately when the sort option is selected.
the task list to re-sort immediately upon selection
I do not experience delays or confusion.
the user selects a sort option
the Done list shall re-sort immediately.

16.title: Clickable Observation ID Opens Task Details

content.text: Observation IDs in the grid shall be displayed as clickable links. On click, the system shall open the Task Details screen for the selected observation in a new view
Observation IDs in the grid to open task details
I can review complete information of a completed task.
the system shall open the Task Details screen in a new view
The top of the screen shall display breadcrumb text Maintenance / Task Details to indicate navigation context.
breadcrumb text displayed on the Task Details screen
I can understand my navigation path and location within the system.
the user opens the Task Details screen
breadcrumb text Maintenance / Task Details shall be displayed at the top.

18.title: Display Three Tabs in Task Details

content.text: The Task Details screen shall display three tabs: Observation, Testing, and Timeline.
see three tabs in the Task Details screen
I can access Observation, Testing, and Timeline information easily.
the system shall display the tabs: Observation, Testing, Timeline.
Breadcrumb Maintenance / Task Details shall be shown at the top of the page.
the breadcrumb to appear consistently on the Task Details screen
the navigation context is always clear.
the Task Details screen is open
breadcrumb Maintenance / Task Details shall be shown.
Switching between tabs shall not modify underlying data; it only changes what is displayed.
switching between tabs to not modify any underlying data
I can safely review task information without altering records.
the user switches between Observation, Testing, and Timeline tabs
no stored data shall be modified; only the display changes.
All information in Observation and Testing tabs shall be view-only for the inspector/assignee (no inline edits).
all information in the Observation and Testing tabs to be view-only
I cannot accidentally modify task data.
the Observation or Testing tab is open
the user interacts with the fields
all fields shall be read-only with no inline edits allowed.
When the Observation tab is active, the system shall display a Location Details section at the top-left.
a Location Details section displayed when viewing an observation
I can see exactly where the issue occurred.
a Location Details section shall appear at the top-left.
Location Details shall show: Rack Name, Bay ID, Element, and Location.
the Location Details section to show key identifiers
I can understand the precise location of the observation.
it shall show: Rack Name, Bay ID, Element, Location.
The Location value shall be a clickable link (e.g., “Front Side – Bay 06 – Level 1”) that opens the rack layout / plan view for that location.
the Location field to be clickable
I can open a detailed rack layout or plan view.
the system shall open the rack layout or plan view for that location.
Below Location Details, the system shall show an Observation Summary section.
I can review core details of the observation.
the system shall show an Observation Summary section below Location Details.
Observation Summary shall display: Observation, Activity Phase, Severity, ID, Inspection Type, Flag, and Notes / Comments.
the Observation Summary to show all essential observation data
I can fully understand the context of the issue.
Severity shall be displayed as a coloured  pill/chip (e.g., RED, AMBER, GREEN).
severity to appear as a colored pill or chip
I can easily recognize the severity level.
severity shall appear as a coloured chip (RED, AMBER, GREEN).
Flag shall show one or more labels (e.g., “High Urgency”, “Severity Change”) as chips.
important markers like urgency or severity changes are visually highlighted.
each flag shall appear as a chip (e.g., High Urgency, Severity Change).
Notes / Comments shall show the inspector’s written description of the issue.
I can understand detailed descriptions of the issue.
the Notes / Comments field shall display the inspector’s written description.
The system shall display a Measurement table within the Observation tab.
a Measurement table in the Observation tab
I can review all measured values relevant to the issue.
a Measurement Table shall be displayed.
The table shall have columns: Measurement Name, Measured Value, and Result.
the Measurement table to show structured information
I can easily interpret measurement results.
it shall include: Measurement Name, Measured Value, Result.
Result column shall show Pass/Fail as labelled chips or text.
measurement results shown clearly as Pass or Fail
it shall show Pass/Fail as labelled chips or text.
A Reported Information section shall display below the Measurement table.
I can see who reported the issue and when.
a Reported Information section shall appear below it.
Reported Information shall include: Reported By (name), Designation, and Reported On (date & time).
The Observation tab shall show narrative fields on the right or bottom: Possible Reason, Risk, Corrective Action, and Remarks.
narrative fields like Reason, Risk, Corrective Action, and Remarks displayed
I can understand the detailed engineering thought process.
Possible Reason, Risk, Corrective Action, Remark

36.title: Narrative Fields in Rich Text Format

content.text: Each of these fields shall display multi-line rich text entered by the inspector/engineer.
narrative fields displayed in rich-text format
they shall appear as multi-line rich text and shall be read-only.
These narrative fields shall be read-only in this view.
narrative fields to be read-only in the Observation tab
I cannot accidentally modify critical recorded notes entered by the inspector or engineer.
the user is viewing the Observation tab
narrative fields (Possible Reason, Risk, Corrective Action, Remarks) are displayed
the fields shall be read-only and no inline editing shall be allowed.
On the right side, the system shall display a Media Evidence panel.
a Media Evidence panel displayed on the right side
I can view all supporting media associated with the observation.
the system shall display a Media Evidence panel on the right side.
Media Evidence shall include three sections: Images, Videos, and Audio.
the Media Evidence panel to include Images, Videos, and Audio sections
I can easily access all types of evidence.
the panel shall include Images, Videos, and Audio sections.
The Images section shall show thumbnails of all photos linked to the observation.
all linked images displayed as thumbnails
I can quickly preview visual evidence.
image files are linked to the observation
thumbnails for all images shall be displayed.
The Videos section shall show video thumbnails with a play icon overlay.
video thumbnails to show a play icon overlay
I can identify which media are videos.
each video thumbnail shall display a play icon overlay.
The Audio section shall show an inline audio player with play/pause and duration.
audio files to be playable via an inline player
I can listen to audio evidence directly.
the system shall show an inline audio player with play/pause and duration.
Clicking any image/video thumbnail shall open a larger preview (lightbox or full-screen viewer).
open media in a larger preview
image or video thumbnails are displayed
the system shall open a larger preview (lightbox or full screen).
Media files shall be view-only; delete or edit actions shall not be available in this screen.
all media files to be view-only
evidence cannot be edited or deleted.
no edit or delete actions shall be available.
When the Testing tab is active, the system shall display a Testing Summary section.
the Testing tab to show a Testing Summary
I can review testing requirements quickly.
a Testing Summary section shall be displayed.
Testing Summary shall show a label “TESTING REQUIRED” followed by a Yes/No indicator chip.
a clear Yes/No indicator showing whether testing is required
I can understand the testing status immediately.
the system displays the testing status
it shall show: TESTING REQUIRED followed by a Yes/No indicator chip.
When a task is in Done status and the user clicks Timeline, the system shall open the Timeline screen for that task.
the Timeline screen to open when viewing Done tasks
I can review historical actions and events.
the task is in Done status
the user clicks the Timeline tab
the system shall open the Timeline screen for that task.
The screen shall display breadcrumb navigation Maintenance / Task Details.
breadcrumb navigation shown on the Timeline screen
I know where I am within the system.
the user opens the Timeline screen
breadcrumb text Maintenance / Task Details shall be displayed.
The system shall ensure the Timeline screen is view-only; no edit actions shall be available.
the Timeline screen to be read-only
the historical workflow cannot be altered.
the user is viewing the Timeline
no edit controls shall be available; all timeline data is view-only.
The system shall display all workflow events in a vertical timeline with dots representing each event.
workflow events displayed in a vertical timeline
I can visually track progress through the task lifecycle.
the system shall display them in a vertical timeline with dots for each event.
The timeline shall list events in reverse chronological order (latest event at the top, earliest at bottom).
timeline events listed with the latest event at the top
I can immediately see the most recent action.
events shall appear in reverse chronological order:

52.title: Mark Most Recent Event as Active

content.text: The most recent event (Task Closed) shall be marked with an active/filled dot.
the most recent event marked visually
I can easily identify the current or last action taken.
it shall be marked with an active/filled dot.

The timeline shall show the following events if they exist for the task: Task Closed, Work Completed, Task Reassigned, Work Completed (new assignment), Task Assigned, Action Assigned, Observation Raised.
the timeline to show all workflow-related events
I can understand the full history of the task.
it shall display the following events (if they exist):
Each event shall display the event title, performed by, event timestamp, and event comments.
each timeline event to show essential event metadata
I can understand who performed the action and when.
The Task Closed event shall include: “By”, “Notes/Comment”, and date/time.
Task Closed events to show detailed closure information
I can see who closed the task and why.
it is displayed in the timeline
The system shall show “Work Completed” entries whenever a user marks the task as completed.
Work Completed events displayed whenever a task is marked as completed
I can trace completion actions throughout the workflow.
a task is marked as completed
a Work Completed event shall appear.
Each entry shall contain: By, Notes/Comment, Timestamp.
each Work Completed event to show essential metadata
the completion activity is fully documented.
a Work Completed event is displayed
The system shall show Task Reassigned events with fields “By” and “Notes/Comment”.
Task Reassigned events to show By and Notes/Comment
I can understand who reassigned the task and why.
the system shall show a Task Reassigned event with fields:

59.title: Show Reassignment Events Every Time Task Is Reassigned

content.text: Reassignment events shall appear in the timeline every time the task is assigned to a new user.
every reassignment to appear in the timeline
I can track the full history of ownership changes.
the task is reassigned multiple times
each reassignment shall appear as a separate timeline event.
The system shall display Task Assigned events with fields: “By”, Assigned To, and “Notes/Comment”.
Task Assigned events to show key assignment information
I can see who assigned the task and to whom.
The system shall display “Action Assigned” with fields: By, Action Replace (Action Type), Severity Change, Notes/Comment.
Action Assigned events to show action details
I can understand what action was taken and severity changes.
The system shall display the Observation Raised event including: “By”, “Notes/Comment”, and timestamp.
the Observation Raised event to show who created the observation and when
I can understand how the issue originated.
The system shall display a Media Evidence panel on the right side of the screen.
a Media Evidence panel on the right side
I can view linked images, videos, and audio evidence.
the timeline or task details screen is open
a Media Evidence panel shall appear on the right side.
The panel shall contain three sections: Images, Videos, and Audio.
the Media Evidence panel divided into Images, Videos, and Audio
media is organized and easy to navigate.
The Images section shall display all uploaded images as thumbnails in a horizontal grid.
all uploaded images shown as thumbnails in a grid
all images shall display as thumbnails in a horizontal grid.
The Videos section shall display playable thumbnails using the video icon overlay.
video thumbnails with a play icon
I can easily identify and play video evidence.
each video shall display as a playable thumbnail with a video icon overlay.
The Audio section shall display an inline audio player with play/pause and timer.
audio files playable with an inline player
I can listen to audio evidence directly from the panel.
it shall show an inline audio player with play/pause and timer.
Clicking any image or video thumbnail shall open a full-screen preview.
open any image or video in full-screen preview mode
I can inspect media details clearly.
image or video thumbnails are shown
the system shall open a full-screen preview.
All media elements shall be read-only, with no delete/edit options.
evidence cannot be altered or deleted.
the user interacts with the media
no delete or edit options shall be available.
If timeline data fails to load, the system shall show an error message: “Unable to load task timeline. Please try again.”
an error message displayed when timeline data cannot be loaded
I understand that the system encountered an issue.
“Unable to load task timeline. Please try again.”

71.title: Update Maintenance List When Task Is Done

content.text: When a task is marked DONE in Task Details → Timeline, the system shall automatically update the Maintenance list view.
the Maintenance list to update automatically when a task is marked DONE
the timeline or task details update
the Maintenance list view shall automatically update.
The system shall refresh only the affected row instead of reloading the entire table for performance optimization.
only the affected row to refresh when a task changes status
the system performs efficiently without reloading the entire table.
only the affected row shall refresh (not the whole table).
When the task status changes to DONE, the Status column shall show the DONE badge in green colour.
the Status column to show a green DONE badge
the Status column shall display a green DONE badge.
The DONE status badge shall be non-interactive (view-only)
the DONE badge to be non-interactive
users cannot modify the final state of a completed task.

75.title: Automatically Move Task to Done Tab

content.text: The task shall automatically move from its previous tab (In Progress / Review) to the Done tab when its status becomes DONE.
tasks to automatically move to the Done tab when status becomes DONE
tasks appear in the correct workflow stage.
a task status updates to DONE
the task shall move from its previous tab (In Progress/Review) to the Done tab.
Searching for the Task ID shall immediately reflect the DONE status in results after completion.
search results to reflect updated DONE status immediately
I can find the updated task instantly.
the user searches for a Task ID
the task has been marked DONE
the search results shall show the DONE status immediately.
Priority indicator (flag colour) shall remain visible even after task is DONE.
the priority indicator to remain visible even after the task is DONE
I can understand the original severity level of completed tasks.
it appears in the Done tab or search results
the priority flag/colour indicator shall remain visible.
